description: In October 2012, Iceland held a non-binding national referendum on a new draft constitution written by a popular assembly after the 2008 financial crisis. While 67% voted 'Yes', the parliament failed to formally enact it.

key_events.description: Constitutional Council publishes the final version of the draft.
Main political parties announce they will not be bound by the referendum result.
Grassroots 'Yes' campaign gains traction on social media.
Voters go to polls, reflecting frustration with political establishment.

calibration_notes: A classic case of a 'populist' constitutional project versus institutional skepticism.
